message:
fix(layout): make pane coordinate rounding honor explicit tie-break policy

`scale_div_round` previously had an implicit "round half up" tie
break that ran for *every* rounding policy except
`TowardNegativeInfinity`. This meant
`NearestHalfTowardNegativeInfinity` — the only other variant today
— was effectively "round half up" rather than "round half toward
negative infinity", which contradicts its own name and makes pane
pointer normalization give the wrong cell for exact tie inputs like
`CssPixels { position: (15, 0) }`.

Refactor the tail of `scale_div_round` to match on `rounding`
explicitly:

- `TowardNegativeInfinity` — no rounding adjustment; keep the
  Euclidean floor.
- `NearestHalfTowardNegativeInfinity` — only adjust when
  `twice_remainder > den`, i.e. strictly past the halfway point.
  Exact ties deliberately keep the floor, which corresponds to
  rounding toward negative infinity, matching the variant name.

## Test updates

The existing `positive_tie` case already covered "positive tie stays
at 1" and is preserved. A new `positive_above_tie` case covers
`position: (16, 0)` to prove that *past* halfway we still round up
to cell 2 — i.e. the fix does not accidentally turn every positive
input into a floor. The existing `negative_tie` case continues to
cover the negative-direction semantics.

## Why this matters

Pointer hit testing for subpixel CSS pixel inputs lives on top of
this path, so an off-by-one on a tie rounded the wrong way directly
produces "clicked between two cells and landed on the wrong one"
bugs for applications that use fractional coordinate inputs.
